properties.title: Premium Azure Files Storage Account Quota Management

options.text: Configure the Storage Quota Manager to automatically increase FSLogix file share quotas before they fill up.

elements.name: subscription
elements.label: Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription where the Storage Quota Manager function app will be deployed.

elements.name: associateWithHostPool
elements.label: Associate with a Host Pool
elements.toolTip: Enable to associate this deployment with an AVD host pool for tagging and automatic naming convention detection. Disable for standalone storage scenarios such as App Attach.

elements.name: hostPool
elements.label: Host Pool
elements.toolTip: Select the host pool whose FSLogix storage accounts should be managed.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('basics').hostPoolsApi.value, (hp) => parse(concat('{"label":"', hp.name, '","description":"', first(skip(split(hp.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"id":"', hp.id, '","name":"', hp.name, '","location":"', hp.location, '"}}')))]

transforms.list: sort_by(value, &displayName)[?name!='global' && !contains(displayName, '(Stage)')] | [*].{label: displayName, value: { displayName: displayName, name: name }}

elements.name: location
elements.label: Deployment Region
elements.toolTip: Select the region where the function app will be deployed.

options.text: No host pool is associated. Resource naming cannot be derived automatically. You must provide custom names for the Function App and Storage Account on the Advanced page.

elements.name: storageSubscription
elements.label: Storage Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the FSLogix storage accounts to manage. Defaults to the deployment subscription.

elements.name: storageResourceGroup
elements.label: Storage Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the resource group containing the FSLogix storage accounts. Defaults to the resource group from the host pool's storageResourceGroupId tag when a host pool is selected.

elements.name: functionAppResourceGroup
elements.label: Function App Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the resource group where the Storage Quota Manager function app will be deployed. Defaults to the storage resource group.

elements.name: useExistingHostingPlan
elements.label: Use Existing App Service Plan
elements.toolTip: Select an existing App Service Plan instead of creating a new one.

elements.name: appServicePlanSubscription
elements.label: App Service Plan Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription that contains your app service plan.

elements.name: resourceGroup
elements.label: App Service Plan Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the shared operations group containing resources used to manage all host pools.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('infrastructure').resourceGroupsApi.value, (rg) => parse(concat('{"label":"', rg.name, '","description":"location: ', rg.location, '","value":{"location":"', rg.location, '","name":"', rg.name, '","id":"', rg.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: appServicePlan
elements.label: App Service Plan
elements.toolTip: Select the existing App Service Plan. Only plans in the selected subscription and location are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('infrastructure').serverFarmsApi.value, (sf) => equals(sf.location, steps('basics').location.displayName)), (sf) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sf.name, '","description":"Location: ', sf.location, ' | Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(sf.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', sf.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: planSku
elements.label: App Service Plan SKU
elements.defaultValue: P0v3 (1 vCPU, 4 GB RAM) - Most Cost-Effective
elements.toolTip: Select the SKU for the new App Service Plan. P0v3 is recommended for cost optimization and fully supports all required features (Always On, VNet integration, PowerShell 7.4).

allowedValues.label: P0v3 (1 vCPU, 4 GB RAM) - Most Cost-Effective

elements.name: zoneRedundant
elements.label: Enable Zone Redundancy
elements.toolTip: Deploy the App Service Plan with zone redundancy. Only applies when creating a new plan.

options.text: Configure encryption for the function app storage account.

elements.name: keyManagementStorageAccounts
elements.label: Storage Account Key Management
elements.toolTip: Choose between platform-managed or customer-managed keys for storage encryption.

elements.name: keyVaultSubscription
elements.label: Key Vault Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the Key Vault with customer-managed encryption keys. For brownfield deployments, this allows selecting Key Vaults from centralized security subscriptions.

elements.name: encryptionKeyVault
elements.label: Encryption Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Select the Key Vault containing the customer-managed encryption key. Only Key Vaults in the selected location are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('infrastructure').keyVaultsApi.value, (kv) => equals(kv.location, steps('basics').location.name)), (kv) => parse(concat('{"label":"', kv.name, '","description":"Location: ', kv.location, ' | Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(kv.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', kv.id, '"}')))]

options.text:
Zero Trust Networking for Function Apps and Azure Resources

This section configures network-level security controls to enforce Zero Trust Architecture principles for the Session Host Replacer function app and its dependencies (storage accounts, key vaults).

Private Endpoints

Enable private connectivity to Platform as a Service (PaaS) resources (Blob Storage, File Storage, and Key Vaults) by assigning private IP addresses from your virtual network. This removes public internet exposure and routes traffic over the Microsoft backbone network.

Virtual Network Integration

The function app requires outbound virtual network integration to communicate privately with resources protected by private endpoints. This requires a dedicated subnet with Microsoft.Web/serverFarms delegation. Both features require Private DNS Zones for proper name resolution. For centralized DNS management in hub-and-spoke topologies, refer to the Private Link hub-and-spoke deployment guidance linked below.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | Private Link Deployment in a Hub and Spoke Network

options.text: When deploying function app zero trust networking, you must select the subnet and Private DNS Zones required for DNS resolution. The available virtual networks are dynamically filtered according to the regions specified for the resource types in earlier configuration screens. The drop-down menus will present only the virtual networks, subnets, and Private DNS Zones that match your prior selections, ensuring that you can choose the appropriate resources for your Zero Trust implementation.
Subnets

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network to which the function app will be integrated. The list is filtered based on the subscription and location selected on the 'Deployment Basics' page.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('infrastructure').virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('basics').location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Location: ', vnet.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: functionAppOutboundSubnet
elements.label: Function App Outbound subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for function App Outbound access. This subnet must have a delegation for Microsoft.Web/serverFarms.

elements.name: functionAppPrivateEndpointSubnet
elements.label: Function App Private Endpoint Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select the subnet for function app private endpoints (inbound access and storage account). This subnet must NOT have any delegations and must be different from the outbound subnet.

options.text: Private DNS Zones

To connect privately with your function app and private endpoints, you need DNS records. We recommend that you integrate your function app private endpoints with private DNS zones. If you select the checkbox below, you'll be asked to select the appropriate Private DNS Zone for each resource type deployed by this solution and the deployment will automatically create the Private DNS integration when creating the private endpoints. Alternatively, you can assign policies to private endpoints to automatically register the endpoints with the appropriate private DNS zones. Uncheck the box below if that is the method you are using.

elements.name: enablePrivateDNSIntegration
elements.label: Enable Private DNS Zone Integration
elements.toolTip: To connect privately with your private endpoint, you need a DNS record. Check this box to select the appropriate private DNS zones so that the deployment automatically integrates each private endpoint with Azure private DNS.

elements.name: privateDNSZonesSubscription
elements.label: Private DNS Zone Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription that contains your Private DNS Zones.

elements.name: azureFunctionAppPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Azure Function App
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for Azure Function Apps

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('infrastructure').privateDNSZonesApi.value, (zone) => or(startsWith(zone.name, 'privatelink.azurewebsites.'), startsWith(zone.name, 'privatelink.appservice.'))), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"', zone.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(zone.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', zone.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: azureBlobPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Azure Blob Storage
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for Azure Blob Storage.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('infrastructure').privateDNSZonesApi.value, (zone) => contains(zone.name, 'privatelink.blob.')), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"', zone.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(zone.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', zone.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Permitted IP Addresses / CIDR Ranges (optional)

Restrict public access to PaaS resources (storage accounts, function app) to specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Use this when managing the deployment from a trusted workstation outside the Azure network boundary. Leave empty to allow all public traffic (or rely solely on private endpoints if deployed).

elements.name: permittedIPs
elements.ariaLabel: Permitted IP Addresses or CIDR Ranges
elements.label: Permitted IP Addresses / CIDR Ranges

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid IPv4 address or CIDR range.

elements.name: enableMonitoring
elements.label: Enable Monitoring for the Function App and AVD Insights
elements.toolTip: Deploy the required resources to enable AVD Insights monitoring.

elements.name: subscription
elements.label: AVD Monitoring Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription where existing AVD monitoring resources are located

elements.name: logAnalyticsWorkspace
elements.label: Existing Log Analytics Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Log Analytics Workspace to which function app diagnostic logs will be sent.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('monitoring').logAnalyticsWorkspacesApi.value, (la) => parse(concat('{"label":"', la.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(la.id, '/'), 4)), ', Location: ', la.location, '","value":"', la.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Azure Monitor is a constellation of different interconnected services that work together to monitor your workloads. An Azure Monitor private link connects a private endpoint to a set of Azure Monitor resources to define the boundaries of your monitoring network. That set is called an Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS).

link.label: Use Azure Private Link to connect networks to Azure Monitor - Azure Monitor | Microsoft Learn

elements.name: enableAMPLS
elements.label: Attach function app Application Insights to an existing Azure Monitor Private Link Scope

options.text: Infrastructure resource names are derived from the hpNamingConvention tag on the host pool. Use 'Use Custom Naming Overrides' below to explicitly define individual resource names for brownfield scenarios.

The hpNamingConvention tag was not found on the host pool. This is expected if the host pool was not deployed with this solution. Infrastructure resource names will use default CAF naming values unless you provide overrides below.

elements.name: enableCustomNaming
elements.label: Use Custom Naming Overrides
elements.toolTip: Check to explicitly define resource names for brownfield scenarios. When unchecked, names are derived from the hpNamingConvention tag on the host pool (or CAF defaults if the tag is absent).

options.text: Infrastructure resource names are derived from the host pool naming convention and the identifier below. With identifier 'desktop-01' and the default CAF convention, names follow the pattern: [rt]-[identifier]-sqm-[unique]-[location] (e.g., fa-desktop-01-sqm-abc123-eus). Verify the identifier matches your host pool.

elements.name: identifier
elements.label: Identifier
elements.toolTip: Host pool base name used to name per-host-pool SQM resources (Function App, Storage Account). Pre-populated from the hpIdentifier tag on the host pool. Maximum 16 characters.

constraints.validationMessage: Identifier must be 0-16 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens only.

elements.name: enableCustomAbbreviations
elements.label: Customize Resource Type Abbreviations
elements.toolTip: Override the default CAF resource type abbreviations used when computing infrastructure resource names (e.g. 'fa' for Function Apps, 'asp' for App Service Plans). Only needed if your organization uses non-standard abbreviations that differ from CAF defaults.

elements.name: functionApps
elements.label: Function Apps
elements.toolTip: Resource type abbreviation for Function Apps. Default CAF value: fa

elements.name: appServicePlans
elements.label: App Service Plans
elements.toolTip: Resource type abbreviation for App Service Plans. Default CAF value: asp

elements.name: userAssignedIdentities
elements.label: User Assigned Identities
elements.toolTip: Resource type abbreviation for User Assigned Identities. Default CAF value: uai

elements.name: privateEndpoints
elements.label: Private Endpoints
elements.toolTip: Resource type abbreviation for Private Endpoints. Default CAF value: pe

elements.name: networkInterfaces
elements.label: Network Interfaces (PE NICs)
elements.toolTip: Resource type abbreviation for Network Interfaces (used for Private Endpoint NICs). Default CAF value: nic

options.text: These names will be automatically derived from your host pool name. To override any of them, enable 'Use Custom Naming Overrides' above.
No host pool associated — automatic naming preview is unavailable. You must provide custom names on the Advanced page.

[if(empty(steps('basics').hostPoolProps.tags.hpNamingConvention), 'Convention not detected (hpNamingConvention tag absent on host pool). Names shown below are approximate based on host pool name pattern.', concat('Convention detected from hpNamingConvention tag. Identifier: ', coalesce(steps('basics').hostPoolProps.tags.hpIdentifier, '(not set)')))]

[concat('Function App: ', if(or(endsWith(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-vdpool'), endsWith(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-hp')), concat(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-sqm-{unique}-', steps('basics').location.name, '-fa'), concat('fa-', steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-sqm-{unique}-', steps('basics').location.name)))]
[concat('Storage Account: ', toLower(replace(concat(steps('basics').hostPool.name, 'sqm{unique}', steps('basics').location.name), '-', '')))]

[concat('Storage Encryption Identity: ', if(or(endsWith(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-vdpool'), endsWith(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-hp')), concat(steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-sqm{unique}-storage-encryption-', steps('basics').location.name, '-uai'), concat('uai-', steps('basics').hostPool.name, '-sqm{unique}-storage-encryption-', steps('basics').location.name)))]
These names are used for the Azure Function App and its supporting resources. All fields are required when custom naming is enabled.

elements.name: functionAppNameOverride
elements.label: Function App Name
elements.toolTip: Required. Explicit name for the Function App. Must be globally unique in the azurewebsites.net namespace, 2-60 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens. Example: func-avdsqm-prod-eus2

validations.message: Function app name must be 2-60 characters, containing only alphanumeric characters and hyphens.

elements.name: appServicePlanNameOverride
elements.label: App Service Plan Name
elements.toolTip: Optional. Explicit name for the App Service Plan. Only applies when creating a new plan (not using an existing one). The ASP is shared across add-ons in the region — leave blank to use the convention-derived name (e.g., asp-avd-eus). Must be 1-40 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens.

validations.message: App Service Plan name must be 1-40 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens only.

elements.name: storageAccountNameOverride
elements.label: Storage Account Name
elements.toolTip: Required. Explicit name for the Storage Account (used by Function App). Must be globally unique in Azure, 3-24 characters, lowercase alphanumeric only. Example: stavdsqmprod

validations.message: Storage account name must be 3-24 characters, lowercase alphanumeric only (no hyphens or special characters).

elements.name: storageEncryptionIdentityNameOverride
elements.label: Storage Encryption Identity Name
elements.toolTip: Optional. Explicit name for the user-assigned identity used for storage account CMK encryption. Only relevant when Customer-Managed Keys are selected. Leave blank to derive from naming convention. Example: uai-avdsqm-prod-encryption-eus2

validations.message: Identity name must be 3-128 characters, alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.
